generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance JointCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring input current of robot joint"
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorAngularVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring motor angular velocity of robot joint"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorAngularVelocityDataTransmitted
]

instance AxisPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring first‑axis position of the robot"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AxisPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance JointTorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring joint torque during machining"
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointTorqueDataTransmitted
]

instance JointTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring joint temperature"
    DTDFVocab:producedData JointTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance PayloadSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring payload mass carried by the robot"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PayloadDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Data transmitted from sensors
instance JointCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointCurrentSensor
]

instance MotorAngularVelocityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorAngularVelocitySensor
]

instance AxisPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AxisPositionSensor
]

instance JointTorqueDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointTorqueSensor
]

instance JointTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom JointTemperatureSensor
]

instance PayloadDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PayloadSensor
]

// C10: Data objects (raw traces)
instance JointCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw current measurement trace from joint current sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData JointCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessingEnabler
]

instance MotorAngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw angular velocity trace from motor angular velocity sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorAngularVelocityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessingEnabler
]

instance AxisPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw position trace from first‑axis position sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData AxisPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessingEnabler
]

instance JointTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw torque measurement trace from joint torque sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData JointTorqueDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessingEnabler
]

instance JointTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw temperature trace from joint temperature sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData JointTemperatureDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessingEnabler
]

instance PayloadData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw payload mass trace from payload sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PayloadDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PreprocessingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers (processing & infrastructure)
instance PreprocessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filter preprocessing component that removes noise from raw sensor traces"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance MQTTBrokerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT broker using lightweight publish‑subscribe, JSON payloads and hierarchical topics"
    DTDFVocab:enables CentralDataMemoryService
]

instance AASExecutorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell executor that incorporates incoming traces into the digital shadow and links them to simulation models"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, CentralDataMemoryService
]

instance ProcessingComponentEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component that consumes raw traces, runs simulation or ML models and emits derived data"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService, OnlineControlSupportService, ProcessOptimizationService, QualityPredictionService, CO2EmissionPredictionService
]

instance ThingsboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Thingsboard IoT platform storing digital‑shadow data and exposing it via APIs"
    DTDFVocab:enables CentralDataMemoryService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink engine executing the physics‑based (white‑box) friction dynamics model"
    DTDFVocab:enables ProcessOptimizationService, OnlineControlSupportService
]

instance MLModelEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Engine handling online training and inference of the neural‑network (black‑box) friction predictor"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService, OnlineControlSupportService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time monitoring of joint friction and sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides MonitoringInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance by detecting friction anomalies"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnomalyInsight, ScheduleMaintenanceAction
]

instance OnlineControlSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides compensated friction parameters to robot controller"
    DTDFVocab:provides CompensationInsight, AdjustFrictionParameterAction
]

instance ProcessOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulates machining parameters, identifies critical subprocesses for optimisation"
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessOptimizationInsight, OptimizeMachiningParametersAction
]

instance QualityPredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predicts product quality"
    DTDFVocab:provides QualityInsight
]

instance CO2EmissionPredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predicts CO₂ emissions"
    DTDFVocab:provides CO2EmissionInsight
]

instance CentralDataMemoryService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Central data‑memory service that other applications can query"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataMemoryInsight
]

// C17: Insights
instance MonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing continuous monitoring of joint friction"
]

instance FrictionAnomalyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight detecting friction anomalies for predictive maintenance"
]

instance CompensationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight delivering compensated friction parameters for the robot controller"
]

instance ProcessOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight identifying critical subprocesses for process optimisation"
]

instance QualityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight predicting the quality of the machined product"
]

instance CO2EmissionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight predicting CO₂ emissions of the machining process"
]

instance DataMemoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight exposing stored digital‑shadow data for external queries"
]

// C17: Actions
instance AdjustFrictionParameterAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically apply compensated friction parameters to the robot controller"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ScheduleMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Trigger a maintenance workflow based on detected friction anomalies"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance OptimizeMachiningParametersAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatically adjust machining parameters according to optimisation insight"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C10: Models / Data
instance WhiteBoxFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics‑based friction dynamics model implemented in MathWorks Simulink (white‑box)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance BlackBoxFrictionPredictor : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data‑driven neural‑network predictor for friction, retrained online (black‑box)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MLModelEnabler
]

instance SysMLStructureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML block diagram capturing system structure, properties and interfaces"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AASExecutorEnabler
]

instance ADLComponentConnectorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component‑connector architecture model described in ACME/AADL/MontiArc"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AASExecutorEnabler
]

instance StateMachineBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "State machine model representing robot behavior"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AASExecutorEnabler
]

instance FMUModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Functional Mock‑up Unit exporting simulation models for integration"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler, AASExecutorEnabler
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twin is synchronised with the physical robot in real‑time by an AAS Executor that continuously streams measured joint data to the digital shadow stored in ThingsBoard. The approach is designed to meet the low‑latency and dead‑time constraints of the robot control loop, keeping the temporal granularity of the digital model identical to the sampling rate of the physical sensors (continuous data stream)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system follows a component‑based orchestration where the robot is described by a SysML structural model, its friction behaviour by executable Simulink/FMI simulation models, and both are encapsulated in an Asset Administration Shell. An AAS Executor orchestrates the data flow: it pulls live sensor data via MQTT, runs the simulation models, and writes measured, simulated and reference values into the ThingsBoard IoT platform that serves as the persistent digital shadow. The pipeline is automated and provides fine‑grained traceability from raw data traces to processing components and models, while resource management is handled by the AAS container and the ThingsBoard back‑end."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "According to the documents, the Digital Twin lifecycle follows the typical Model‑Based Systems‑Engineering (MBSE) development chain: (1) capture of requirements, (2) functional architecture modelling with SysML internal block diagrams, (3) logical architecture expressed with component‑&‑connector ADLs such as ACME, AADL or MontiArc, (4) physics/product‑technical layer realised by simulation or code (e.g., Simulink, C++/Java), and (5) operational stage where the twin executes active processing components that consume data traces and generate higher‑level abstractions. Transition between stages is supported by explicit traceability links (the “originates‑from” association) that allow a data trace to be linked back to its originating processing component, input traces and the models used. Governance is achieved through a reference‑model/conformance‑relation approach: a Digital Shadow Reference Model defines concepts, and concrete realizations must conform to it via a mapping of incarnations to the reference classes."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is ensured by traceability from source data traces through processing components to resulting simulation or data‑calculation models, as defined in the digital‑shadow reference model. Validation includes verification of model transformations (e.g., SysML to Simulink) and runtime comparison of predicted friction behavior against measured sensor streams to detect anomalies. Uncertainty is addressed by continuously updating model parameters or retraining neural‑network models as new data arrive, thereby compensating for wear, temperature variations, and payload changes. The approach emphasizes verification of transformation correctness, dynamic adaptation to maintain accuracy, and use of the digital twin as a central data memory for quality‑assurance checks."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection relies on MQTT as the core communication protocol. MQTT provides a lightweight, message‑based, publish‑subscribe architecture with hierarchical topics; messages are untyped JSON strings. The broker can assign read/write permissions per topic, enabling controlled access. Data from the MQTT broker are consumed by the AAS Executor, which further forwards the assembled digital shadow to the open‑source ThingsBoard platform for storage and visualization. No explicit network topology, bandwidth, latency, redundancy, or security mechanisms (e.g., TLS, VPN) are described in the documents."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with external systems is realised through MQTT for IoT messaging, ThingsBoard for data storage and visualisation, and the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) as an Industry 4.0 interoperability standard. Simulation models are linked via the Functional Mock‑up Interface (FMI) to combine Simulink behavioural models with the SysML structural model. These standards and protocols enable seamless data exchange with other enterprise systems and allow the digital shadow to be extended by additional applications."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The described approach relies on established standards for modelling and interoperability: SysML (OMG), AADL (SAE), ACME (software architecture), MontiArc, and Simulink are used to describe structure and behaviour. Conformance relations to a reference class diagram enforce a standardised model vocabulary (Documents 2 and 4). For runtime data exchange, the MQTT protocol (implemented by Mosquitto, Document 6) and the ThingsBoard IoT platform are employed, providing compliance with common IoT communication standards. No specific industry certification or ISO/IEC standards are mentioned."
]
